column1: # Principle: Forth-style new-word definitions via `def NAME [body]`,
#            body ops marked `/s` for true stack-only semantics.
# Classic Forth defines new words with a colon definition:
#     : square dup * ;
#     : cube  dup dup * * ;
#     : -rot  rot rot ;
# In aqleng the equivalent is `def NAME [body]` — a list-bound def
# whose body is spliced in-place at every use site (NOT an `fn` call
# with named parameters; see fn.tsv for that). The body executes
# against the current stack, exactly like a Forth colon definition.
# THE `/s` MODIFIER (CRITICAL FOR FORTH SEMANTICS)
# Production Forth's `+`, `*`, `-`, etc. are purely stack-based —
# they only ever consume args from the stack, never look ahead at
# future tokens. aqleng's default math ops (addq, subq, mulq) are
# FORWARD-PRECEDENCE: at a body's splice site, an inner mulq's
# fwd-scan can greedily consume the next literal in the outer
# stream. So a naive `def square [ dup mulq ]` invoked as
# `3 square 4` does NOT compute `3*3=9 leaving 4 on stack` — the
# body's mulq sees `4` as a valid Number forward arg and grabs it,
# producing `3*4=12`.
# The fix is the `/s` call-site modifier: a forward-prec word with
# `/s` appended forces stack-only dispatch (BarrierPos=0 override),
# so it cannot fwd-scan past its own splice. This recovers the true
# Forth semantics — exactly what `dup * ;` does in real Forth.
# Convention used throughout this file
# Inside `def NAME [body]` bodies, every forward-prec op gets the
# `/s` suffix:
#     def square [ dup mulq/s ]    ← like Forth's `: square dup * ;`
#     def 1+     [ 1 addq/s ]      ← like Forth's `: 1+ 1 + ;`
#     def fneg   [ 0 swap subq/s ] ← like Forth's `: negate 0 swap - ;`
# Stack-only ops (dup, swap, drop, over, rot, nip, tuck, 2dup, …)
# don't need /s — they already only see the stack. Only forward-prec
# ops get the suffix.
# What this file does NOT cover
# Words that require `if`/`then`/`else` or `for`/`while` are
# deliberately omitted. abs, max, min, factorial all need
# conditionals which aqleng's spec runner doesn't yet register.
# They land once `if`/`for` core words land. See section 9.
# tokens
# ---------- 1. Increment / decrement / scale ----------
def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] 5 1+
column3: classic Forth `: 1+ 1 + ;` — addq/s body op consumes from stack only

column1: def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] 0 1+
def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] -3 1+
def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] 5 1+ 1+
def 1- [ 1 subq/s ] 5 1-
def 1- [ 1 subq/s ] 1 1-
def 1- [ 1 subq/s ] 0 1-
def 2+ [ 2 addq/s ] 5 2+
def 2- [ 2 subq/s ] 5 2-
def 2x [ 2 mulq/s ] 5 2x
column3: `: 2* 2 * ;` — Forth's classic `2*` (renamed 2x because the spec tokenizer treats `2*` poorly)

column1: def 2x [ 2 mulq/s ] -3 2x
# Mid-stream chaining: with /s the body doesn't grab the next literal.
def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] 1 1+ 2 1+
column3: 1 1+ → 2 (consumes only the 1); 2 1+ → 3; final stack: [2 (residue from second), 3]. Wait actually trace: 1 1+ → splice → 1 1 addq/s → addq/s stack=[1,1] sig=[1,1] → 2; stream becomes "2 2 1+"; second 1+ splices → "2 2 1 addq/s" → addq/s stack=[2,2,1] sig=[1,2] → 3; final: "2 3"

column1: def double [ dup addq/s ] 5 double
column3: `: double dup + ;` — addq/s pulls both from stack

column1: def double [ dup addq/s ] 7 double
def double [ dup addq/s ] 0 double
def square [ dup mulq/s ] 5 square
column3: `: square dup * ;` — mulq/s consumes only the dup'd top

column1: def square [ dup mulq/s ] -4 square
column3: negative input still squares to positive

column1: def square [ dup mulq/s ] 0 square
# THIS IS THE KEY ROW — proves `/s` makes square work properly when
# followed by another forward-prec call. Without /s, the inner mulq
# would grab the `4` and produce 147 (= 3 + 12*12 + residue). With
# /s, the body is closed: 3 square = 9, 4 square = 16, addq combines.
def square [ dup mulq/s ] 3 square 4 square addq
column3: chained squares: with mulq/s neither square grabs the next literal; 9 + 16 = 25

column1: def cube [ dup dup mulq/s mulq/s ] 4 cube
column3: `: cube dup dup * * ;` — both mulqs are stack-only

column1: def cube [ dup dup mulq/s mulq/s ] 3 cube
def cube [ dup dup mulq/s mulq/s ] -2 cube
# ---------- 3. Sum-of-squares (Pythagorean a²+b²) ----------
# Classic Forth `: sumsq dup * swap dup * + ;` — given (a b) on stack,
# computes a²+b². Useful as the body of `: hyp sumsq sqrt ;` once
# sqrt exists. All three forward-prec ops in the body get /s.
def sumsq [ dup mulq/s swap dup mulq/s addq/s ] 3 4 sumsq
def sumsq [ dup mulq/s swap dup mulq/s addq/s ] 1 1 sumsq
def sumsq [ dup mulq/s swap dup mulq/s addq/s ] 5 12 sumsq
def sumsq [ dup mulq/s swap dup mulq/s addq/s ] 0 0 sumsq
# ---------- 4. Stack-manipulation words from primitives ----------
# Classic Forth defines composite stack ops in terms of lower-level
# primitives. These rows verify that aqleng's core stack ops behave
# identically to their Forth-textbook definitions. None of these
# bodies use forward-prec ops, so no /s suffix is needed — dup, swap,
# drop, over are already stack-only.
# `: 2dup over over ;` — duplicate top pair via two `over` calls.
# Trace [a, b]: over → [a, b, a]; over → [a, b, a, b].
def 2dup-alt [ over over ] 1 2 2dup-alt
column3: classic `: 2dup over over ;` — pure stack ops, no /s needed

column1: def 2dup-alt [ over over ] "x" "y" 2dup-alt
def 2dup-alt [ over over ] 1 2 2dup-alt 2dup
# `: nip swap drop ;` — drop second-from-top, keeping the top.
# Trace [a, b]: swap → [b, a]; drop → [b]. Matches core `nip`.
def nip-alt [ swap drop ] 1 2 nip-alt
def nip-alt [ swap drop ] 1 2 3 nip-alt
# `: tuck swap over ;` — copy the top under the second.
# Trace [a, b]: swap → [b, a]; over → [b, a, b]. Matches core `tuck`.
def tuck-alt [ swap over ] 1 2 tuck-alt
# Classic Forth: `: -rot rot rot ;` — pure stack, no /s.
# Trace [a, b, c]: rot → [b, c, a]; rot → [c, a, b].
def -rot [ rot rot ] 1 2 3 -rot
def -rot [ rot rot ] "a" "b" "c" -rot
def -rot [ rot rot ] 0 1 2 3 -rot
# ---------- 6. Negation via `0 swap subq` ----------
# Classic Forth: `: negate 0 swap - ;`. The body's subq/s consumes
# only the swapped (0, n) pair from the stack — n becomes 0-n = -n.
# Trace [n]: 0 → [n, 0]; swap → [0, n]; subq/s sig=[n (top), 0] →
# handler b-a = 0-n = -n.
def fneg [ 0 swap subq/s ] 5 fneg
def fneg [ 0 swap subq/s ] -3 fneg
def fneg [ 0 swap subq/s ] 0 fneg
def fneg [ 0 swap subq/s ] 7 fneg
5 negq
column3: control: bare negq for the same input

column1: # `and` and `or` are forward-prec with BarrierPos=1, so an inner
# `and`/`or` could grab a forward token in mid-expression. Use /s
# in the body for true stack-only Forth semantics. `not` is also
# forward-prec; same fix.
def both-true [ and/s ] true true both-true
def both-true [ and/s ] true false both-true
def both-true [ and/s ] false true both-true
def either [ or/s ] true false either
def either [ or/s ] false false either
def neither [ or/s not/s ] false false neither
column3: classic NOR via composition: `or not`, both stack-only

column1: def neither [ or/s not/s ] true false neither
column3: or fires first (true), then not flips it

column1: # ---------- 8. Composition: word built from defined words ----------
# Each splice happens at use site; the inner def must be established
# BEFORE the outer is invoked. With /s on the body's forward-prec
# ops, composition works cleanly even when literals appear between
# defined-word calls.
# square applied twice — quartic.
def square [ dup mulq/s ] def quart [ square square ] 3 quart
def square [ dup mulq/s ] def quart [ square square ] 2 quart
def square [ dup mulq/s ] def quart [ square square ] -2 quart
# Increment-twice composing a defined `1+`.
def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] def 2incr [ 1+ 1+ ] 5 2incr
def 1+ [ 1 addq/s ] def 4incr [ 1+ 1+ 1+ 1+ ] 5 4incr
# Square-then-double — pipeline through two defined words.
def square [ dup mulq/s ] def double [ dup addq/s ] 5 square double
# Sum-of-squares of two squared values via independent squares + addq.
def square [ dup mulq/s ] 3 square 4 square addq
column3: square 3 → 9; square 4 → 16; addq → 25 (works WITHOUT paren-wrap because mulq/s closes the body)

column1: # ---------- 9. Limits — what NEEDS conditionals ----------
# Words requiring `if`/`then`/`else` or `for`/`while` aren't yet
# expressible. Documented below as a punch list:
#   : abs    dup 0 < if negate then ;       — needs if/then
#   : max    2dup < if swap then drop ;     — needs if/then
#   : min    2dup > if swap then drop ;     — needs if/then
#   : sign   dup 0 < if drop -1 else
#                       0 = if 0 else 1 then then ;  — needs if/else
#   : factorial dup 1 = if drop 1
#                          else dup 1- recurse * then ;  — needs recurse/if
# Once the engine grows `if`/`then`/`for`, these classic idioms can
# land — and will live in this file alongside the conditional-free
# rows above.
